title: T-069 — verify organizations.industry_code CHECK accepts 'generic' (FT-009)
prompt:
# T-069 — verify organizations.industry_code CHECK accepts 'generic' (FT-009)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §4.3
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-009** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P2** (XS effort)

## Goal
Idempotently ensure 'generic' is in the organizations.industry_code CHECK whitelist and add a regression test.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration 027 inspects pg_constraint; if 'generic' is missing, ALTER TABLE drops and recreates the check.
2. Test inserts an organization row with industry_code='generic' and asserts success.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/__tests__/industry-code-generic.test.ts [create]
- packages/db/migrations/027-industry-code-generic-verify.sql [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 027 applied, when an org is inserted with industry_code='generic', then insert succeeds.
2. Given migration 027 is re-run, when applied to a DB that already has 'generic' in the check, then it is a no-op (idempotent).

## Test strategy
- RED: write industry-code-generic.test.ts inserting industry_code='generic'; should fail before migration 027 if 'generic' was missing.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/db migrate && pnpm --filter @monopilot/db test industry-code-generic`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not drop the check entirely.
- Do not break existing industry_code values.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Verify (and if missing, add) 'generic' to the organizations.industry_code CHECK whitelist.
pipeline_inputs.details: T-014 introduced industry_code with a CHECK constraint enumerating supported codes. The seed planner assumes `'generic'` is in the whitelist; this task verifies and, if absent, idempotently adds it via migration 027.
out_of_scope: Do not add other industry codes in this task.

Given migration 027 is re-run, when applied to a DB that already has 'generic' in the check, then it is a no-op (idempotent).
